scenario: A BPMN model has subprocesses in collapsed state (visual). Team wonders if deployment includes inner elements or just collapsed view.
question: Subprocess collapsed/expanded -- deployment behavior?

options.text: In BPMN, the collapsed or expanded visual state is stored in the diagram interchange (DI) section of the XML, while the executable logic of the subprocess is in the main process definition. The engine reads only the logical structure and completely ignores the DI visual properties. Deployment always includes all inner elements regardless of visual presentation, so the collapsed view has no effect on what gets deployed or executed.

The BPMN XML structure is identical whether the diagram shows a collapsed or expanded view; only the isExpanded attribute in the DI section changes. The Zeebe engine does not read DI attributes for execution. A collapsed subprocess and its expanded counterpart deploy as the exact same executable process with no behavioral difference whatsoever.

Visual state (collapsed/expanded) doesn't affect deployment: the collapsed/expanded property is purely a diagram rendering hint stored in the BPMN Diagram Interchange section. The Zeebe engine evaluates only the logical process model, not the visual DI properties. All inner tasks, gateways, and flows within a subprocess are always present in the deployed artifact regardless of how the modeler displays the subprocess. This allows teams to use collapsed views for high-level diagrams while the engine executes the full logic.

The collapsed subprocess feature is only relevant for the Camunda Modeler tool and has no significance beyond diagram editing -- this partially understates the point. While it is true that collapse is a modeler presentation feature, the key insight is broader: it is not just a modeler feature but applies to any BPMN tooling that reads the diagram. The engine deploys and executes the full subprocess structure regardless of any tool's visual state.

a.text: Incorrect. Collapsed vs expanded -- presentation only: In BPMN XML: element contains inner elements (flow nodes, sequence flows). in DI section has visual properties. Visual collapsed/expanded: in DI (diagram information). Engine reads logical structure: from main XML. Visual properties: xml ... isExpanded="false" = collapsed visual; doesn't affect execution. Engine perspective: Engine ignores DI section typically. Loads logical model: tasks, flows, gateways. Executes per spec: regardless of how visualised. For modeler workflow (per Set 13 Q5 / Set 15 Q6): Collapse for overview: less clutter. Expand for detail: see internals. Both views same model. For runtime: All inner elements active: process executes them. No "hidden" elements: collapsed doesn't hide from engine. For Operate visualisation: Default view: may show as collapsed. Drill into subprocess: see internals. Same runtime data: just rendering choice. For tooling: Web Modeler: collapse / expand via UI. Desktop Modeler: similar. API-loaded BPMN: engine just reads logic. For tests: Engine behavior: same regardless of visual. Unit tests: don't depend on visual. For BPMN as
